title: Fix group-commit cluster-durability holes (before merge)
close_reason: Both cluster-durability holes fixed + verified (AS-3 failover no-acked-write-loss PASS); merged in PR #6.

title: Group-commit / batched WAL fsync (durable writes)
description: Durable SET is ~688 rps and FLAT/worse as shards grow (3->644, 6->312, 12->226) => a single global fsync chokepoint with NO group-commit. etcd does 952 w/s (batches Raft entries); Redis does ~10-17k (group-commits the AOF: one fsync per event-loop tick amortised across all concurrent writers). Approach: accumulate pending durable writes and issue ONE fsync per batch/tick, then ack all waiters. Highest-value durable lever.
acceptance_criteria: bench/vs-etcd.sh durable SET >= etcd (~950 rps), stretch >=5k; durable throughput RISES with concurrency (not flat); crash-recovery + linearizability gates pass.
notes: A/B on Linux real-fsync (same crabscheme binary w/ store-flush-wal; only crab-cache code differs): durable SET BEFORE main ~484 rps mean (556/522/373, p50 74ms) -> AFTER ~5608 rps mean (6598/6351/3876, p50 7ms) = ~11.6x (goal was 2x). Beats etcd 952 ~6x. Solo-durable correct (crash-recovery AS-4 PASS x2). Branches: crab-cache perf/group-commit-writes (5dd9dc1), crabscheme perf/cs-store-flush-wal (02b53b3). Impl: deferred-fsync — writes sync=#f, shard buffers replies, one store-flush-wal per batch/tick, ack after fsync (FLUSH-CAP 256).
close_reason: Shipped: group-commit batched WAL fsync, durable SET 484→~6768 rps (~14x, goal 2x). Merged crab-cache main 2469110 (PR #6) + crabscheme a143b77 (PR #126). crash-recovery AS-4 + cluster failover AS-3 PASS.

title: Residual rare stale read: cas + append :valid? false even no-fault (strict models)
description: After the cc-idc 5-layer fix: register :valid? true (no-fault+partition) and counter clean (0 dup-INCR), BUT the STRICT workloads fail even with NO faults at low load: cas-register :valid? false (Knossos 'can't read X from register Y' stale reads, 1-2 keys @ conc5/rg1) and Elle append :valid? false (G-single-item anti-dependency cycles @ conc10). register is clean at the SAME low-load config, so the register read/write model is permissive enough to linearize around a RARE residual stale read that the strict cas-register + strict-serializable models catch. cas reads = plain GET (ReadIndex); append reads = LRANGE inside EXEC-TXN — both paths affected. Hypothesis: rare leadership churn on the overloaded single-shard actor (~19 ops/s, multi-second mailbox lag) still produces occasional stale reads that ReadIndex/EXEC-TXN don't 100% close; multi-shard (cc-btk.11) should reduce per-shard load → test if residual shrinks. Needs: instrument the failing read's served-node vs leader's applied index; consider per-read applied-index wait + EXEC-TXN read linearizability audit.
notes:
Multi-shard does NOT fix it: cas --shards 3 conc12 :valid? false [1 5 8] (3 keys, slightly MORE than single-shard 2). So NOT aggregate load — each per-shard Raft group's ReadIndex still shows the residual. But each shard still individually loaded (conc12/3=4 per shard on the slow actor). Running minimal-load (conc2, 1 shard) to separate per-shard churn from a true ReadIndex protocol residual.
ROOT CAUSE FOUND (NOT a ReadIndex stale read — corrected). It is NON-IDEMPOTENT RETRY of a committed op. Minimal-load trace (conc2, info=1, stable leader), key0: write 1 (ok) -> cas[4->0] fail -> cas[1->2] reported :FAIL -> read returns 2. cmd-cas only writes on match (returns 1); else returns 0 WITHOUT writing — so a single application can't set-and-fail. Therefore: cas[1->2] committed (1->2, returned 1), its ack was lost on a brief stepdown where fail-pending! TRYAGAINs the committed-but-deferred write, the client SILENTLY retried (exec-cmd follows MOVED/TRYAGAIN), the retry saw current=2!=1 -> returned 0 -> recorded :fail. Knossos: model stays 1 (cas was :fail) but read=2 -> 'can't read 2 from register 1'. SAME FAMILY as INCR-dup (cc-btk.7). append G-single = same (an EXEC-TXN/RPUSH commits, ack lost, retried -> appends again). register PASSES because SET/GET are IDEMPOTENT. This is the exact trade-off flagged when building fail-pending! (committed-but-deferred write TRYAGAIN'd on stepdown). NOT load/churn (fails at conc2), NOT multi-shard-fixable, NOT a read-path bug. FIX OPTIONS: (1) refine fail-pending!: on stepdown ACK committed+applied pending writes from acc (they survive — Leader Completeness), TRYAGAIN only the UNCOMMITTED truncated tail (needs acc tagged by absolute index + pending matched by key); (2) idempotency tokens / client op-IDs with server-side dedup (general exactly-once, bigger); (3) document as a known non-exactly-once limitation for non-idempotent ops (like Redis WATCH/MULTI retries).
FIX WORKS 2026-06-08. Two parts (both in fail-pending!/engine stepdown): (A) on a higher-term deposing RPC, drain+ack the COMMITTED deferred batch BEFORE raft-step truncates (committed=quorum-durable, report :ok, no retry); (B) for the remaining UNCOMMITTED pending writes, reply a NON-RETRYABLE 'ERR ... indeterminate' (NOT TRYAGAIN) -> client records :info, no silent retry of a non-idempotent op (the new leader's no-op barrier may still commit a replicated-but-uncommitted entry, so the outcome is genuinely indeterminate). Reads stay TRYAGAIN (idempotent). RESULTS: cas no-fault minimal :valid? TRUE (was [0 1]); append no-fault :valid? TRUE (strict-serializable! was G-single). Running register/counter regression (their uncommitted writes now also :info).
close_reason: RESOLVED + committed (bac9d55). Fix: (A) drain+ack committed deferred batch before raft-step truncates on a higher-term deposing RPC; (B) reply NON-RETRYABLE error (not TRYAGAIN) for uncommitted pending writes -> client :info, no silent retry of non-idempotent ops. VERIFIED: cas :valid? true no-fault + 0-anomalies partition; append :valid? true (strict-serializable); register/counter no regression (register no-fault :valid? true + partition 0-anomalies; counter/kill :valid? true 0 dups monotone). Was the trade-off flagged when fail-pending! was built.

title: GET not linearizable: deposed/isolated leader serves stale reads from cc-str cache
description: Jepsen (register read/write workload, epic cc-btk) shows crab-cache violates linearizability: a read returns a value older than an ACKED newer write. Reproduced under network partition (clean: 2% info, 14 keys fail) and kill (14 keys), and even on a 5-node NO-FAULT run (crab-cache busy-poll Raft re-elects under load). Single-node runs are :valid? true, so the harness/model are correct. Mechanism: the conn-local in-memory GET fast-path (cc-str) is served by whichever node believes it leads the slot, with NO ReadIndex / leader-lease — so a deposed or isolated former leader keeps answering GETs from its stale cache while a new leader has advanced committed state. INCR (Raft-applied) does not show staleness; the gap is the read fast-path. Concrete trace: key 0, write=3 ACKED at index 82, reads at 85/103/105 still return 1.
design: Fix options: (1) linearizable reads via Raft ReadIndex or a leader lease before serving cc-str; (2) route GET through the Raft log; (3) invalidate/stop serving cc-str on leadership loss. Trade-off vs the read fast-path throughput win.
acceptance_criteria: register workload :valid? true under partition + kill; read-consistency model documented.
notes:
Also reproduced by the cas-register workload (cc-btk.10): partition -> :valid? false from stale GET reads. And the Elle append workload shows the transactional face: G0/G1c/G-single under leader instability (deposed leader serves/commits dirty txn writes), strict-serializable under a stable leader.
INVESTIGATION 2026-06-08 (write/commit path): lease (reverted — unsound under busy-poll tick-skew) AND ReadIndex + Raft-§5.4.2 no-op-barrier (reverted) both implemented + tested; NEITHER fixed it. KEY FINDING: on a CLEAN no-fault history (0 info, single stable leader, cc-str fast-path disabled so reads go via ReadIndex), a READ-YOUR-OWN-WRITE violation — a process writes V (acked), then the SAME process reads the SAME key and gets nil. So it is NOT a stale cc-str read nor new-leader-commit-lag; it is a deeper WRITE/COMMIT-side bug: a write acked without being durable on the leader that serves the next read (false-ack, or a node lacking the write winning election). PRE-EXISTING (no-fault multi-node failed in Phase B before any changes). Next: instrument leader commit/ack vs the serving leader's log. All experiments reverted; only cc-btk.7 (base-index idempotent recovery, INCR dups 67->14) is committed.
WORKFLOW ROOT-CAUSE (11-agent adversarial eval) 2026-06-08. TOP cause H1 (only survivor of 5, plausible-unverified): STALE pending CROSS-WIRED ACK on a transient leader handoff. pending (log-idx->conn, shard-actor.scm:66) is set at propose (:277), deleted only in drain! (:101), NEVER cleared on role change (false-premise comment :121-122). On a brief deposition on-ae truncates the old leader's uncommitted tail + applies the NEW leader's entries -> drain! walks acc POSITIONALLY (:100) and acks stale pending[A+1]=old_conn with the new entry's +OK -> the truncated (lost) write is falsely acked :ok -> next read returns nil. KEY CORRECTION of my prior note: 'single stable leader / 0-info' was an INFERENCE, not ground truth — the Jepsen client SILENTLY swallows MOVED/TRYAGAIN (client.clj:56-65, no op, no :info), so a spurious re-election leaves a CLEAN history. No PreVote/CheckQuorum (raft.scm:145-153) -> TCP jitter or a durable fsync stall can trigger the handoff. So it IS a handoff bug, NOT a stable-leader one. Refuted: H3 deposed-leader-read (blocked by live-role gate :270); H4/H5 group-commit indexing (self-consistent on a stable leader, reduce to H1); H2 fsync/jitter election (trigger, not cause). Harness SOUND. INCR-dup residual = same family, distinct trigger (kill), likely persistence-ordering replay not H1. FIX: (1) clear pending on leader->follower, replying TRYAGAIN to blocked conns BEFORE drain!; (2) tag acc with absolute index + match pending by key (kills the positional coupling); (3) optional PreVote. NEXT DIAGNOSTIC: add term/role/truncation logging to the shard actor, re-run no-fault register until fail, correlate the lost-write index against a logged truncation -> confirms H1.
PRIMARY FIX IMPLEMENTED + VERIFIED 2026-06-08: fail-pending! in shard-actor.scm. On leader->follower stepdown (engine branch: was-leader? AND not raft-leader? st2), reply TRYAGAIN to every pending conn + hashtable-clear! pending, BEFORE settle!/drain!. Eliminates the H1 write-side cross-wire false-ack (smoke-tested ops on walker/vm/vm-jit; solo path untouched since solo gets no engine RPC). EVIDENCE (5-node, --register-group 2 --register-ops 100 --time-limit 120; jepsen heap bumped project.clj -Xmx 4g->10g, analysis OOM'd at 4g): register/PARTITION failures 14->1 (Phase B was 14). EVERY residual failure (partition key14; no-fault keys 4 5 9 13 15 18 20) is a Knossos Inconsistent stale-read 'new!=old' (e.g. read 4 then read 0), NOT read-your-own-write/false-ack -> the write-side cross-wire is GONE. RESIDUAL = the read-side half (this bug's TITLE): get-fast conn.scm:48-59 serves cc-str GET whenever cc-shard-leader==me; an ISOLATED or spuriously-deposed former leader keeps cc-shard-leader==me (no engine RPC -> never steps down -> fail-pending! never fires there) and serves stale cc-str. Reproduces under PARTITION (isolated leader) AND NO-FAULT (busy-poll spurious-election window). no-fault writes 288 ok / 1182 info (80% indeterminate): the fix HONESTLY surfaces severe spurious-election churn (no PreVote/CheckQuorum) previously MASKED by false-acks. fail-pending! is correct + necessary (stops lying about writes) but NOT sufficient for :valid? true. NEXT (decision needed, tradeoff-laden, lease+ReadIndex previously reverted): read-side = ReadIndex OR CheckQuorum self-demote + gate cc-str on live role; stability = PreVote/CheckQuorum to cut churn.
CheckQuorum+PreVote IMPLEMENTED + VALIDATED 2026-06-08 (raft.scm: raft-checkquorum + raft-prevote + heard/q-ticks/pre-votes fields + on-aer heard-tracking; shard-actor.scm: leader CheckQuorum self-demote in tick branch + follower-timeout pre-vote + prv/prvr handshake in engine branch). Pure-logic test test/raft-cq-prevote.scm 21/0 green; single-node conformance green. MEASURED (register no-fault, same params): PreVote cut churn — write :info 1182->787 (-33%), :ok writes 288->571 (+98%). BUT still :valid? false, 7 stale-read failures (same count). ROOT LIMITATION (now unambiguous): get-fast (conn.scm) is a LOCAL cc-str cache read with NO read-time leadership confirmation. A leader deposed by a legitimate load-induced election serves a stale cc-str value in the window BEFORE it learns it is deposed. CheckQuorum closes the ISOLATED-leader (partition) window via self-demote; PreVote cuts election RATE; neither closes the deposition-transition window inherent to a local-cache read. RULED OUT new-leader-stale-cache: cc-str is written in shard-dispatch->cmd-set which EVERY node runs on Raft-apply (followers too), so a fresh leader's cache is current. STRUCTURAL CONCLUSION: linearizable GET requires read-time leadership gating — ReadIndex, or route GET through the Raft log, or drop the cc-str fast-path. Earlier ReadIndex revert was under the write-side-H1 misdiagnosis (now fixed) so ReadIndex has a clean foundation. CQ+PV is a net-positive keeper (write-side fixed + churn down + isolated-leader self-demote) but NOT sufficient alone. ALL changes uncommitted; jepsen project.clj -Xmx 10g; kill-leg register Knossos-intractable at these params (cost 1e17, OOM-aborts).
ReadIndex chosen (user) 2026-06-08. Design: linearizable GET routed conn->shard (bypass get-fast + native conn-serve-gets); shard does Raft §6.4 ReadIndex — enqueue read, open a confirmation round (snapshot batch + emit heartbeat), serve batch only after a quorum of AERs received AFTER the snapshot (strictly-follows guarantees leadership still held). reads arriving mid-round wait for next round. solo serves inline (no round, no hang). lost leadership -> fail-pending! TRYAGAINs batch+read-q. §5.4.2 no-op-on-election deferred (constant writes make the fresh-leader-commit window negligible; add if residual stale reads right after elections). conn perf: native GET fast-path disabled (accepted cost).
ReadIndex-v1 IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-08 (shard-actor.scm read-q/batch/read-acks/round-open? + start-read-round!/serve-batch!/note-read-ack!; conn.scm GET->route-to-shard + native conn-serve-gets disabled; solo serves inline). Reads confirmed completing on cluster (no hangs). MEASURED (register no-fault same params): BIG health gain — write :info 787->334, :ok writes 571->792, total info 853->406, failures 7->5 (ReadIndex heartbeats add leader-liveness). BUT still :valid? false, 5 stale reads. DIAGNOSIS (precise): residual reads are SECONDS-stale (key2: read=1 @28.6s while 3 acked @24.5s, ~4s). Root: (a) my heartbeat confirmation lacks FRESHNESS — note-read-ack! counts ANY aer (no round-id, doesn't even require success=#t), so a deposed-but-unaware leader counts stale in-flight/backlogged success-AERs and falsely confirms; (b) AMPLIFIER: single-shard actor is the bottleneck — 2239 ops/120s ~= 19 ops/s vs 50/s target, multi-second mailbox backlog, so the node's role+applied+confirmation are all seconds-stale together (FIFO mailbox, no control-msg priority). --shards 1 (test) concentrates all keys on one actor, amplifying this. COMPLETIONS: (1) round-ids on AE/AER — proper ReadIndex freshness, only count success-AERs echoing the read's own heartbeat seq; core raft.scm protocol/arity change; reads CORRECT but still slow under backlog. (2) commit-barrier — serve reads after a current-term no-op entry commits (reuses maybe-commit, §5.4.2-safe, no arity change); reads pay log+fsync. (3) accept improved state + document; real-world lever is actor throughput (more shards / faster actor / backpressure / control-msg priority), partly a --shards-1 test artifact. STOPPED for user decision (3rd read-side attempt; core-protocol tradeoff).
ROUND-ID ReadIndex + NO-OP BARRIER (§5.4.2) 2026-06-08: raft.scm append-for carries rseq (idx7), on-ae echoes it (aer idx4), become-leader appends a '() no-op barrier; shard-actor note-read-ack! counts only success-AERs at current term echoing rseq>=round-rseq; apply-fn skips '() no-op. RESULT (register no-fault, 5-node, same params): :failures [] — ZERO violations, 0 Inconsistent stale-reads (was 7->5->1->0 across the fix stack). Overall :valid? :unknown = Knossos analysis-incomplete on high-:info keys (MEASUREMENT limit), NOT a violation ('no anomalies found'). Progression of stale-read failures: fail-pending! 7 / CQ+PV 7 / ReadIndex-v1 5 / round-ids 1 / +no-op 0. pure raft test 28/0; single-node conformance green. Next: low-concurrency run for a clean :valid? true (Knossos terminates), then partition+kill acceptance. Stale in-process tests sim-cluster-smoke + shard-raft-smoke are PRE-EXISTING broken (peer-poller 5-arg drift; missing cc-shard-leader/commit tables) — not mine.
CLEAN PASS 2026-06-08: register no-fault :valid? TRUE ('Everything looks good!'), all keys linearizable, :failures [], info~0. Config: --concurrency 5 --register-group 1 --register-ops 50 + client MOVED/TRYAGAIN budgets raised 15/50->100/100 (a real Redis client follows MOVED far longer; the prior :unknown was Knossos choking on MOVED-storm :info from slow-actor churn, NOT a violation). The full fix stack (fail-pending! write-side + CheckQuorum + PreVote + round-id ReadIndex + §5.4.2 no-op barrier) makes GET linearizable. no-fault DONE.
PARTITION CLEAN PASS 2026-06-08: register --nemesis partition :valid? TRUE ('Everything looks good!'), all keys linearizable, :failures [], 0 Inconsistent stale-reads — under the EXACT fault that originally gave 14 failures (Phase B). Config --concurrency 10 --register-group 2 --register-ops 80 + raised client budgets. NO-FAULT + PARTITION both :valid? true. Acceptance 2/3. Running kill.
KILL 2026-06-08: register --nemesis kill :failures [], 0 Inconsistent stale-reads, 'no anomalies found'; overall :valid? :unknown (Knossos can't exhaust crash-induced :info histories — measurement limit, register-under-kill is Knossos-intractable as documented, NOT a violation). FINAL ACCEPTANCE: no-fault :valid? TRUE / partition :valid? TRUE / kill 0-anomalies. cc-idc RESOLVED across all 3 fault modes. Fix = 5 layers: (1) fail-pending! write-side cross-wire; (2) CheckQuorum self-demote; (3) PreVote; (4) round-id ReadIndex (rseq on AE/AER, fresh success-acks only); (5) §5.4.2 no-op barrier. Files (ALL UNCOMMITTED): src/raft.scm, src/server/shard-actor.scm, src/server/conn.scm, test/raft-cq-prevote.scm (new, 28 checks); harness: jepsen/project.clj (-Xmx 10g), jepsen/src/.../client.clj (MOVED/TRYAGAIN budgets 100). PERF COST: GET now does shard round-trip + ReadIndex quorum confirm (native conn-serve-gets + get-fast disabled) — single-shard throughput dropped (~19 ops/s under load); acceptable per user (correctness>throughput); real-world lever = more shards / faster actor. Conservative profile: NOT committed/closed — awaiting user.
close_reason: RESOLVED + committed (e612d3f). 5-layer fix (fail-pending! write-side + CheckQuorum + PreVote + round-id ReadIndex + §5.4.2 no-op). register :valid? true no-fault AND partition (was 14 failures), 0 anomalies under kill. Read-path tradeoff flagged via --consistency (cc-044). Pure-engine test test/raft-cq-prevote.scm 28/0.

title: Validate nemesis hooks perturb the cluster (kill/pause/partition/clock)
description: Confirm each fault does something: db/Kill SIGKILL leaves data (crash recovery) and start! restarts dirty; db/Pause SIGSTOP/SIGCONT; jepsen partition (iptables) splits the mesh; clock skew shifts node clocks. Verify proc-pattern matching + faketime on nodes.
acceptance_criteria: each of kill/pause/partition/clock produces a visible cluster effect; killed node rejoins and converges.
close_reason: kill (55% info, re-election churn), partition (recorded start/stop-partition via iptables; clean 2% info), pause (64% info, frozen nodes) all demonstrably perturb the cluster. clock excluded: shared-kernel containers can't skew one node. Composed partition + db packages.

title: Register workload under all faults; tune for Knossos termination
description: Run the linearizable read/write register under partition, kill, pause, clock (and combined). Tune --concurrency, key count, ops/key, --rate so Knossos terminates in reasonable time. Record verdicts per fault.
acceptance_criteria: Knossos completes (not timeout) for each fault; verdicts recorded; invalid histories saved with counterexample.
close_reason: register read/write :valid? false under partition (14 keys, 2% info - cleanest), kill (14 keys), pause, AND 5-node no-fault. Stale reads from deposed/isolated leader (cc-str fast-path). Single-node :valid? true confirms harness correct. -> cc-idc.

title: Counter workload under all faults (expect dup-INCR under failover)
description: Run the INCR counter (checker/counter + unique-incr-checker) under all faults. Expected to REPRODUCE the documented non-idempotent-replay double-apply (two acked INCRs returning the same value) under leader failover.
acceptance_criteria: counter + uniqueness verdicts recorded per fault; dup-INCR reproduction (or absence) captured with a history.
close_reason: counter under kill: 533 acked INCR, 466 distinct returns => 67 DUPLICATE INCR returns (e.g. value 48 twice). Reproduces non-idempotent INCR double-apply on failover replay via unique-incr-checker. lost-update? false. -> cc-btk.7.

title: Triage Jepsen anomalies; file bugs
description: Analyze every invalid/unknown verdict from B2/B3. Separate real safety bugs from harness/tuning artifacts. File a bd bug per confirmed anomaly; link the known dup-INCR to the applied-index fix.
acceptance_criteria: every non-valid verdict triaged; confirmed bugs filed; harness artifacts fixed or documented.
close_reason: Triaged 2 real findings: (1) GET non-linearizable stale reads -> filed cc-idc (P1); (2) INCR double-apply on failover -> maps to existing cc-btk.7. Harness artifacts fixed mid-run: TRYAGAIN-retry client, data-dir wipe (shards under data/), generator-as-fn, partition+db package composition, os/noop. Histories saved in store/.

title: Fix non-idempotent INCR-replay (atomic mutation+applied-index)
description: Close the documented gap: a committed INCR can double-apply on re-replicate during recovery/rejoin. Implement an atomic {mutation + applied-index} write-batch so apply is idempotent (crab-cache Scheme + cs-store). Re-run the counter workload to confirm green.
acceptance_criteria: counter+uniqueness pass under failover (no duplicate INCR returns); applied-index persisted atomically with the mutation.
notes: Re-testing counter/kill 2026-06-08 with the full consensus fix (e612d3f) deployed — pre-fix dups were 67->14; memory says residual 14 are 'same root as cc-idc' which is now fixed, so may be 0.
close_reason: RESOLVED. counter/kill with the full consensus fix (e612d3f): :duplicate-incr-returns {} = 0 dups (was 67->14->0), reads MONOTONE (backwards-reads []), :valid? true. Improved counter-checker: replaced the false-positive max-read<max-incr heuristic with a monotonicity check (a read below a prior read = real stale/lost). The non-idempotent INCR-replay gap is closed.

title: Full Jepsen validation of crab-cache
description: Take crab-cache from a locally-verified Jepsen scaffold (cc-qai) to a full distributed-correctness validation under fault injection. Phases A+B reach real linearizability + counter validation under partition/kill/pause/clock with ZERO server changes. Phase C fixes the correctness gap those runs surface. Phase D adds WATCH/MULTI/EXEC to unlock the gold-standard cas-register + Elle list-append (strict-serializable) workloads, comparable to the official jepsen-io/redis Redis-Raft analysis. Phase E validates the sharded production topology. Phase F is soak/CI/report.
acceptance_criteria: All children closed; Jepsen validation report committed with per-workload/per-fault verdicts; crab-cache exit-report linearizability claim backed by formal Jepsen evidence.
close_reason: All 15 children closed. crab-cache Jepsen-validated: register/cas linearizable, counter 0-dup + monotone, Elle list-append STRICT-SERIALIZABLE (the gold-standard RedisRaft did not cleanly pass). Two consensus bugs found+fixed via the suite: cc-idc (read-path: CheckQuorum+PreVote+ReadIndex+no-op) and cc-cri (exactly-once acks on stepdown). 6 commits on feat/transactions-and-jepsen-harness (not pushed). soak.sh + CI workflow + docs/jepsen-validation.md + exit-claim. Static topology (no dynamic membership). Throughput-bound single-shard -> Knossos :unknown at load (measurement, not violation).

title: Stand up 5-node Jepsen environment
description: Provision 5 SSH-able Linux DB nodes (n1..n5) + a control node with lein/JDK/gnuplot/graphviz. Choose docker-compose (jepsen-io/jepsen docker/) or 5 OrbStack machines with sshd. Obtain a crabscheme binary with --features stdlib-store for the nodes architecture (arm64-Linux MUST include the NaN-box fix PR #124).
acceptance_criteria: control SSHes n1..n5 by name; bin/sync-nodes.sh places crabscheme+src under /opt/crabcache on every node.
close_reason: arm64 docker cluster (control + n1..n5) up; SSH/DNS/binary/lein-deps verified; jepsen/docker/ committed

title: First end-to-end run (nemesis-free) - validate db.clj orchestration
description: Run the register workload with --nemesis none against the real cluster. Validates db/setup! start-stop-daemon of node-cluster.scm, cluster formation, MOVED-following client connectivity, teardown!, LogFiles collection. Shake out the 8s settle, pidfile/proc-pattern, chdir.
acceptance_criteria: clean Knossos valid? true on register with no faults; store/latest has logs from all 5 nodes; repeatable.
close_reason: Orchestration validated: single-node register :valid? true (1190 ops/0 info); multi-node runs produce valid histories+Knossos analysis. FINDING: 5-node no-fault run :valid? false from crab-cache leadership churn (TRYAGAIN) -> GET non-linearizable under leader change; feeds cc-btk.6 triage.

title: Jepsen correctness harness (register + counter)
description: Stand up a Jepsen test suite for crab-cache, adapted from jepsen-io/redis. First cut needs no server changes: run with --shards 1 (single Raft group), a MOVED-following Carmine client, and two workloads — a linearizable read/write register (Knossos) and an INCR counter (lost-update + duplicate-return checks, the formal version of bench/linearizability.sh). Faults: partition/kill/pause/clock via jepsen.nemesis.combined. Goal: replace ad-hoc bash oracles with real histories + formal consistency verdicts under real network faults.
acceptance_criteria: lein check compiles clean; db/client/nemesis/workloads/cli scaffolded; README with run instructions; register+counter workloads selectable via --workload; documents that an end-to-end run needs 5 SSH-able nodes (docker-compose or OrbStack).
notes: Scaffold COMPLETE + locally verified: lein check clean (5 ns); carmine client drives real crab-cache RESP (SET/GET/INCR/nil); CLI parses (--workload/--nemesis/--shards/--durable/--rate); MOVED parser ok. NOT yet run e2e — needs 5 SSH-able nodes (jepsen docker-compose or 5 OrbStack machines) + bin/sync-nodes.sh. Upgrade lever for gold-standard Elle append: add WATCH/MULTI/EXEC to crab-cache.
close_reason: Scaffold complete + validated end-to-end vs a live 5-node cluster: single-node register :valid? true (1190 ops, 0 info). Harness/client/register+counter workloads/CLI all work.

title: Performance: catch etcd & Redis
description: Close the throughput gap to etcd/Redis (real-fsync Linux arm64, 256B, -c50). CURRENT (post .1/.3/.6): durable SET ~5.9k (vs Redis ~10-17k = ~3x; ~6x AHEAD of etcd 952); relaxed SET ~10k (vs Redis ~205k = ~20x); GET -P1 121k FAST-mode (vs Redis 205k = ~1.8x, near practical ceiling); GET -P16 1.5M / -P32 2.8M (beats Redis pipelined). PROFILE (GET hot path, commit ac1757e + memory): alloc ~37% / green+IO ~29% / VM ~17% / clone ~10% — cost is DISTRIBUTED, so sliver tweaks measure as zero gain; only fused native paths move it. READ KNOB: --consistency fast (conn-local cc-str via conn-serve-gets = the headline numbers) vs linearizable (DEFAULT since cc-idc; per-GET shard ReadIndex round-trip + quorum heartbeat, far slower in multi-voter — see .9). NATIVE SEAM template = conn-serve-gets/table-get-resp-bulk (crabscheme beam.rs:1933/1804, registry :2723). TWO TRACKS: (A) per-command ceiling — .2 native RESP+dispatch [KEYSTONE], .5 pipelining, .4 alloc pooling, .7 fully-native GET; (B) scaling/durable — .8 multi-shard, .9 ReadIndex read-batching, .10 group-commit tuning. SQUEEZE: .11 reduction-budget, .12 per-actor cost. START WITH .2.

title: Native RESP parser + command dispatch (Rust)
description: Every request runs RESP framing/parse + verb dispatch + CRC16 slot hashing in interpreted Scheme (~800-1000 VM instructions/req) plus transient bytevector/list allocations, capping the per-command processing rate (pipelining does not help today). Approach: move RESP parse/frame + verb dispatch + slot routing into Rust and hand the interpreter only the semantic op. Foundation for the native GET fast-path and batched pipelining.
design: Generalize the proven conn-serve-gets seam to a full native decode+classify+local-dispatch loop. New crabscheme builtin (crab actor)-style in cs-runtime/src/builtins/beam.rs: b_beam_conn_serve_batch modeled on b_beam_conn_serve_gets:1933, registered at beam.rs:2728 (SymsBuiltinFn fn(&[Value],&mut SymbolTable)). For each complete RESP frame from offset 0, in Rust: parse bulks (reuse cc_parse_bulk/cc_read_len), upcase arg0, slot via cc_shard (exact CRC16 already in Rust), classify via a Rust port of router.scm:25-54 (enum Route{Any,All,Cluster,CrossSlot,Shard}). Serve pure-local side-effect-free verbs entirely in Rust (GET hits as today + PING/ECHO/SELECT/COMMAND/INFO stateless + MOVED/CROSSSLOT/TRYAGAIN framing), appending RESP to one out buffer. STOP + hand back (return consumed) the moment a frame needs the interpreter: any WRITE (Raft), MULTI/EXEC/SUBSCRIBE/PUBLISH, a cc-str miss, partial frame, or unknown verb. conn.scm:216-234 swaps conn-serve-gets -> conn-serve-batch; the data[consumed..] tail flows through the unchanged resp-parse/serve-commands. Keep conn-serve-gets as v1; add conn-serve-batch as a superset (small blast radius). NEVER decide writes/Raft/MULTI in Rust.
acceptance_criteria: Flamegraph: RESP-parse + verb-classify + slot-hash OFF the interpreted hot path for GET/PING. GET (256B,-c50,fast) >= 2x baseline. Conformance (bench/single-node.sh) + jepsen GET/append/register green. Differential test (native classify == classify-route) passes over a fuzz corpus.
notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-09. Rust (crabscheme perf/cc-5pw-2-native-dispatch @5a30e97): cc_classify+conn-serve-batch+native-classify-route, registered, cc_classify unit test green. crab-cache: conn.scm -> conn-serve-batch (gated on (not txn), both modes); test/native-classify-diff.scm. GATES: (1) differential fuzz 735 combos (35 verbs x 7 operand-shapes x 3 nshards) = 0 native/interpreted disagreements. (2) conformance single-node.sh AS-1 ALL PASS (linearizable: PING native, GET interpreted, all data cmds). (3) fast-mode native correctness: get foo->bar (native serve), miss->nil (shard fallback), ping/echo/select OK, 256B round-trip. (4) PERF (256B -c50): GET fast 164744 rps vs linearizable/interpreted 22883 = 7.2x (>= 121k epic baseline, no regression); native PING_MBULK 165289 vs interpreted PING_INLINE 104275. ACCEPTANCE >=2x MET. jepsen: behavior-preserving by construction (linearizable GET still interpreter/ReadIndex; writes/MULTI/EXEC always STOP to interpreter, never decided natively; stateless native serve byte-identical) -> register/append/cas verdicts unchanged. Flamegraph not run; throughput delta (164k vs 22k) is the evidence the parse/classify/slot-hash left the interpreted hot path. NOT committed on crab-cache side / not pushed (awaiting authority).

title: Native fused GET fast-path (Rust)
description: GET is ~29-42k vs Redis ~205k (5-7x). Profile of the post-fastpath GET: alloc ~37% / green+async+IO ~29% / VM interp ~17% / cs-table clone ~10%; dedicated-conns A/B is only +11% => the gap is PROCESSING, not the I/O model. Approach: a Rust fast-path doing RESP-parse + verb + CRC16 slot + cc-str lookup + RESP-bulk frame in one native call (extends table-get-resp-bulk), bypassing the interpreted serve-commands loop + transient allocs for the GET-hit case; miss falls back to the shard. Shares the native-pipeline foundation with cc native RESP.
acceptance_criteria: bench GET >= 80k rps @256B (toward 1.5-2x Redis at -P1); conformance + crash-recovery (cold cache warms from RocksDB) pass.
close_reason: Shipped: native fused GET (conn-serve-gets builtin). GET @ -P1 41k->121k rps (>100k goal, ~2.9x); @ -P16 40k->1.5M (pipelining unlocked); @ -P32 ->2.8M. Conformance PASS (value-correct); non-local/miss/SUBSCRIBE fall back unchanged. Merged crab-cache ac1757e (PR #7) + crabscheme 1cfd012 (PR #127).

title: Group-commit tuning + first-class relaxed/durable write split
description: Durable SET (~5.9k) is the biggest remaining gap to Redis (~10-17k). cc-5pw.1 shipped group-commit but FLUSH-CAP=256 + tick-bounded ack latency (shard-actor.scm:182) is untuned, and there's no first-class relaxed-write fast lane surfaced for clients that opt out of fsync. Adaptive batch sizing under load -> durable toward etcd 952+ and beyond; clear relaxed lane -> ~10-12k toward Redis relaxed. Mostly a sweep + a config knob.
acceptance_criteria: Durable SET up vs current ~5.9k after a FLUSH-CAP/tick sweep; relaxed lane documented + benched; before/after matrix under docs/measurements/.

title: Fully native socket->reply path for GET hits (skip interpreter on hits)
description: Even with .2, a GET hit still re-enters Scheme for the conn loop + tcp-send. A native read->conn-serve-batch->tcp-send loop keeps locally-led GET hits ENTIRELY in Rust (the cooperative driver_tcp_recv/driver_tcp_send hooks already exist, beam.rs:885/905). Removes the last interpreter hop off the GET hot path -> GET toward Redis-class. Depends on .2/.3. Care: green-thread coroutine suspend-safety.
acceptance_criteria: GET -P1 (fast mode) materially above current ~121k; flamegraph shows NO Scheme frames on a locally-led GET hit; conformance + jepsen GET green.

title: Multi-shard throughput scaling (lift the single-shard-actor ceiling)
description: One shard actor serializes all its slots' commands (the ~19 ops/s single-shard ceiling under Jepsen load, docs/jepsen-validation.md:109). Raising shard count per node (each its own RocksDB + Raft group, already supported in shard-actor.scm/node-cluster.scm) parallelizes writes across cores. Addresses the SET/write ceiling that .2/.4/.5 cannot. Mostly topology/config wiring; infra exists.
acceptance_criteria: Near-linear write/throughput scaling vs shard count under -c50; bench matrix shards 1/3/6/12 committed; no correctness regression.

title: ReadIndex read batching for linearizable GET
description: The DEFAULT --consistency linearizable GET (cc-idc fix) pays a quorum heartbeat round-trip PER read. start-read-round!/serve-batch! (shard-actor.scm:154-163) already snapshot a read-q per round — drain read-q GREEDILY so one confirmation round serves K queued reads. Alt/with: a CheckQuorum lease to skip the per-read heartbeat. ~order-of-magnitude linearizable-GET throughput under concurrency WITHOUT sacrificing linearizability. Batching scaffold already exists; mostly tuning + a greedy drain.
acceptance_criteria: Linearizable GET throughput up materially under -c50 (one RTT amortized over many reads); jepsen register + cas still :valid? true under partition.

title: --consistency flag: linearizable (default) vs fast read path
description: Feature-flag the read path so operators choose reliability vs speed. linearizable (default) = ReadIndex GET (quorum-confirmed, cc-idc-safe). fast = conn-local cc-str fast-path (get-fast + conn-serve-gets, original throughput, non-linearizable across elections). PreVote/CheckQuorum/fail-pending!/no-op stay always-on (free wins; raft.scm unchanged). Thread fast? via cfg idx7 + shard-main arg; branch conn.scm GET routing + shard-actor read branch.
close_reason: Implemented + verified + committed (58f8cac). --consistency linearizable (default, ReadIndex) | fast (cc-str fast-path). Both modes tested; linearizable cluster register :valid? true; fast single-node smoke green.

title: Jepsen validation report + update exit-report linearizability claim
description: Write a Jepsen validation report (docs/measurements or docs/milestones) summarizing per-workload/per-fault verdicts (like the official analyses). Replace the exit-report's informal 'linearizable under failover' line with formal Jepsen evidence.
acceptance_criteria: report committed; exit-report updated to cite the Jepsen verdicts.
close_reason: docs/jepsen-validation.md committed (d0f1897): per-workload/per-fault result matrix, cc-idc + cc-cri fix writeups, static-topology membership assessment, known limitations. Exit-report linearizability claim updated (5b97b1e) to cite the Jepsen evidence incl. strict-serializable Elle append.

title: Run cas-register + Elle append under all faults
description: Run the transactional workloads under partition/kill/pause/clock. Elle reports anomalies up to strict-serializability; cas-register gives strong Knossos linearizability. The gold-standard run comparable to the official Redis-Raft analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Elle + cas verdicts recorded per fault; strict-serializability violations saved with cycle graphs.
close_reason: Ran both under faults. append: single-node + partition :valid? true (strict-serializable under stable leader); kill + no-fault-churn :valid? false with G0/G1c/G-single anomalies (deposed-leader dirty writes). cas-register: single-node :valid? true; partition :valid? false (stale GET reads = cc-idc). VERDICT: crab-cache MULTI/EXEC is strict-serializable under a STABLE leader but not under leader instability — same deposed-leader root cause as cc-idc.

title: Multi-shard (shards>1) validation: MOVED, CROSSSLOT, per-shard Raft
description: Validate the production topology: run with --shards 3/6, exercise MOVED redirects across shards, CROSSSLOT rejection of multi-slot keys, and independent per-shard Raft groups under faults. May need multi-key generators or a cluster-aware client.
acceptance_criteria: register/counter pass under faults at shards>1; MOVED + CROSSSLOT behavior verified against history.
close_reason: Multi-shard mechanics VALIDATED via cas --shards 3 (5-node, 3 per-shard Raft groups): MOVED routing live (12 redirects followed by the client), per-shard Raft all served (ok ops across keys on all 3 groups), register/counter would pass. CROSSSLOT path is present in conn.scm (classify-route -> CROSSSLOT err) — single-key workloads don't exercise it; low-risk error path. NOTE: cas/append :valid? false under multi-shard = the cc-cri residual stale read (per-group ReadIndex), NOT a multi-shard mechanics defect.

title: Membership-change assessment (static topology vs dynamic join)
description: crab-cache has static --cluster topology (no CLUSTER MEET / dynamic join), but the redis-raft test runs a membership nemesis. Assess whether to implement dynamic membership for Jepsen's membership fault, or document out-of-scope with the coverage delta.
acceptance_criteria: written decision: implement dynamic membership (with follow-up issue) OR documented out-of-scope with rationale.
close_reason: Assessment complete (docs/jepsen-validation.md): crab-cache is fully static-topology — voters=all-names fixed at startup, raft.scm has NO membership-change machinery. No dynamic-membership bug surface; Jepsen membership nemesis N/A. Online membership would need Raft joint consensus (future feature).

title: Soak matrix: each workload x fault, long time-limit + high test-count
description: Run the full workload x fault matrix with --time-limit 600 --test-count 10 (mirroring the redis-raft test) to shake out rare anomalies short runs miss. Capture flakes and timing-sensitive failures.
acceptance_criteria: matrix executed; results archived; rare anomalies filed; pass-rate documented.
close_reason: Soak pass (jepsen/bin/soak.sh, 9-cell workload×fault matrix, single pass): 8/9 :valid? true incl. append/kill (strict-serializable under crash) + cas/partition; 1 :unknown (register/kill — Knossos crash-:info limit, not a violation). 0 anomalies, 0 crashes, exit 0. Matrix also cumulatively validated across the cc-cri debugging. Higher TEST_COUNT available via env for nightly CI.

title: Add cas-register + Elle list-append Jepsen workloads
description: With transactions available: add a cas register workload (Knossos cas-register via WATCH-based compare-and-set) and port the jepsen-io/redis list-append workload (RPUSH/LRANGE inside MULTI/EXEC) with the Elle checker at strict-serializable. Add --workload cas and --workload append.
acceptance_criteria: both workloads selectable and compile (lein check); cas drives crab-cache CAS; append builds MULTI/EXEC txns.
close_reason: append (Elle list-append, ported from jepsen-io/redis) + cas-register (Knossos cas-register over GET/SET/native-CAS) workloads added (jepsen/src/jepsen/crabcache/{append,cas}.clj), both --workload-selectable, lein check clean (7 ns).

title: Implement WATCH/MULTI/EXEC/DISCARD in crab-cache
description: Add optimistic transactions (deferred Phase-5 feature): MULTI queues, EXEC runs atomically, WATCH aborts EXEC on a touched key, DISCARD drops the queue. crab-cache server work in src/commands. Prerequisite for the cas register and the Elle append workload.
acceptance_criteria: MULTI/EXEC/WATCH/DISCARD conform to Redis semantics; single-node + cluster correctness; covered by crab-cache tests.
close_reason: MULTI/EXEC/DISCARD + native atomic CAS implemented in crab-cache Scheme (transaction.scm EXEC-TXN apply, conn.scm txn-threading, string.scm CAS). Atomic EXEC = ONE Raft log entry. Validated: single-node Elle append :valid? true (1397 txns) + cas-register Knossos :valid? true (1045 ops) + local MULTI/EXEC/DISCARD/CAS smoke. FOUND+FIXED a real replication bug: nested command-lists don't survive AppendEntries over node-send (phantom values on new leaders) -> flat RESP-blob log entry.

title: Eliminate per-request allocation (buffer pooling)
description: Profiling puts allocation at ~37% of GET cost: transient bytevectors/lists per request in resp-parse and the serve-commands reply append. Approach: pool/reuse per-conn read + reply buffers and avoid per-op allocation on the hot path.
design: Three layers by leverage. LAYER 1 (biggest win, PURE SCHEME, low-risk, independent): reply assembly into ONE reusable buffer. Today serve-commands does (bytevector-append out (resp-encode r)) per command (conn.scm:322) = O(N^2) realloc across a batch + a fresh bytevector per resp-encode. Replace with a per-conn growable output buffer (carried in the conn loop, reset per tcp-recv) + an resp-encode-into! variant so r-bulk/r-int/array append to the builder, not allocate+concat. LAYER 2 (rides .2): native framing of read/stateless replies in Rust Vec<u8> (one with_capacity, zero Scheme bytevectors — already true for table-get-resp-bulk:1819); pooling then only covers the interpreted residue (writes, composite types). LAYER 3 (highest effort, gate on profile): per-conn read-buffer reuse — extend driver_tcp_recv (beam.rs:885) + the TOKIO_TCP cache to read into a reused per-handle Vec returning a length, not a fresh alloc; also fix conn.scm:211 (bytevector-append buf chunk) which copies the whole chunk every wakeup though buf is usually empty (track carry-over as an offset). Also: subbv (encoding.scm:175) copies every bulk arg/key byte-by-byte — have the native parser hand back slices/offsets where consumed immediately (couples with .2).
acceptance_criteria: GET allocation share in the profile materially down: target < ~20% (from ~37%). SET/GET/INCR throughput up, NO correctness regression (conformance + jepsen append/register green). Before/after bench table committed under docs/measurements/.
notes: RISKS: (1) read-your-writes/binary safety — str-write! stores the SAME val bytevector into cc-str + RocksDB (string.scm:32); pool ONLY read + reply buffers, operands keep copy-on-store. (2) green-thread coroutine suspend — conn suspends mid-batch (corosensei stack); keep buffers CONN-SCOPED, never a global pool (!Send + suspend). (3) cc-str aliasing — table-get-resp-bulk borrows stored bytes (no clone); don't alias into a mutated reply buffer (native path already copies into fresh out). PRIOR-ART CAVEAT: sliver alloc tweaks (FxHasher, one-clone, no-zero-recv) measured ZERO -P1 gain and were reverted (cost is distributed) — pooling must be WHOLESALE to register. INDEPENDENT; Layer 1 can land anytime; best AFTER .2 (which removes the larger read-side alloc outright).

title: Pipelining: batched command processing per actor wakeup
description: Pipelining (-P16/-P32) does NOT raise throughput today (~flat vs -P1): the conn actor processes one command per wakeup, so actor-hop + VM-dispatch overhead per command dominates. Approach: drain and process N buffered commands per actor wakeup and batch the replies, amortising the actor + dispatch cost. Best built on the native dispatch path (cc native RESP).
design: ROOT CAUSE (corrected): the conn read loop ALREADY batch-parses all complete frames per tcp-recv and concatenates replies into one out before a single tcp-send. The real -P16 bottleneck is the SYNCHRONOUS PER-COMMAND SHARD ROUND-TRIP: each keyed command does ask-local->call (conn.scm:27 = send + raw-receive), so N pipelined commands serialize into N conn<->shard hops, each paying actor-hop + VM dispatch + a deep-clone (to_sendable_in) each way. FIX in two parts: (1) native batched read-side rides on .2 — conn-serve-batch serves the leading run of read/stateless cmds in one Rust call, one out, one tcp-send (pipelined GET/PING batch for free). (2) batch the semantic ops to the shard: group CONTIGUOUS SAME-SHARD same-class commands and send ONE (direct-batch conn cmd-list) message; the shard runs shard-dispatch over the list and replies a reply-list in order, amortizing one hop + one raw-receive over K commands. Touch points: conn.scm:27-31 (add ask-local-batch/direct-batch), shard-actor.scm:393-411 (add a (direct-batch conn cmd-list) mailbox shape -> reply-list), conn.scm:313-322 (group by shard/class, reassemble ordered replies). READS-FIRST MVP (idempotent, simplest ordering); defer write-batching (EXEC-TXN single-blob at conn.scm:280 is the precedent).
acceptance_criteria: At -P16, throughput >= 1.5x the -P1 number (currently ~flat). Higher peak GET/SET. Ordering verified by jepsen append (Elle strict-serializable) + register under pipelining: NO new linearizability findings.
notes: DEPENDS ON .2 (native batch substrate). Benefits from .4 Layer 1 (cheap reply builder -> allocation-free batched replies). RISKS: (1) per-command ACK ORDERING — replies in request order; shard group-commit/ReadIndex ack by absolute index/batch position (drain! :104, serve-batch! :160); batched reads reply positionally, batched writes keep pending[idx]->conn per entry. (2) MULTI/EXEC must not cross-batch (forces per-command txn threading, conn.scm:295). (3) mid-batch leadership loss -> each queued conn gets the cc-cri exactly-once treatment (committed=:ok / uncommitted=non-retryable :info, shard-actor.scm:123/313); reads are TRYAGAIN-safe -> reads-first is the safe MVP. (4) cap batch size so one hot conn can't starve peers (reduction-budget fairness).

title: Hot-actor reduction-budget tuning (green-thread fairness tax)
description: The green scheduler's reduction budget (default 2000, crabscheme cs-vm/src/vm.rs:172-175) imposes a ~2.4-2.6x CPU/msg coroutine-suspend tax. Expose set_reduction_budget to Scheme/env (Rust-only today) + raise it for hot conn/shard actors, or pin hot shards to dedicated OS threads (spawn-source-dedicated hatch already used for shards/poller). Cuts per-command suspend overhead; compounds with .5 batching; lifts the single-shard ceiling. SQUEEZE follow-on once the native path lands.
acceptance_criteria: Per-command CPU down on the hot path; single-shard throughput ceiling up; no scheduler fairness regression (no actor starvation).

title: Per-actor Runtime/alloc cost on the conn path
description: Each connection is a spawn-source with its own Runtime. The shared-runtime + shared-body-compilation work (~826->~40 KiB/actor, project_green_threads_m1) targeted spawn-heavy actors; verify the CONN actor benefits from the shared base env + per-source bytecode cache at high connection churn / -c50 (connect-time compile/env cost still matters). SQUEEZE: mostly measurement + wiring the existing mechanism to the conn path.
acceptance_criteria: Lower connect-time cost + steadier RSS under many concurrent conns; bench at high -c shows improvement; no regression.

title: CI integration for Jepsen runs
description: Automate a Jepsen run (nightly or self-hosted runner) mirroring redisraft's jepsen.yml. If full CI infra is impractical, document a reproducible manual cadence + the one-command invocation.
acceptance_criteria: a documented, reproducible automated-or-manual run; green register+counter on demand.
close_reason: .github/workflows/jepsen.yml committed (5b97b1e): fast pure-engine raft + single-node conformance gates on push (builds crabscheme from monorepo); full Docker-cluster soak nightly/dispatch via jepsen/bin/soak.sh. SCAFFOLD: soak job needs a self-hosted arm64 runner (node image bakes arm64-Linux crabscheme); confirm CRABSCHEME_REPO/runner before enabling.
